name: Cheese Pie {Crostata Di Ricotta}
recipeIngredient: 12 tablespoons Lard or butter, at room temperature but not soft.
3 tablespoons White raisins, rinsed and drained
1 egg white mixed with 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. PASTRY CRUST
- In a large mixing bowl, make a well in the center of two cups of flour.
- Drop into it the butter (or lard), egg yolks, sugar, Marsala wine, lemon peel, and salt.
- Mix ingredients together with fingertips, incorporating as much flour as possible.
- Work in the rest of the flour until the dough is smooth and can be gathered into a ball. Do not knead the dough or work it more than necessary.
- If using an electric mixer with a paddle attachment, mix all ingredients at low speed until just combined.
- Refrigerate for about 1 hour or until firm but not hard before rolling out.
2. Shape the remaining dough into a ball and roll out on a lightly floured surface to form a disc about 1 inch thick and at least 11 inches across.
3. Place the pastry in a buttered 9-by-11/2-inch spring-form or false-bottom cake pan, leaving some slack in the center.
4. Press the pastry into the bottom and sides of the pan, trimming excess around the top with a rolling pin.
5. RICOTTA FILLING
- Combine Ricotta cheese with sugar, flour, salt, vanilla, orange peel, and egg yolks until thoroughly mixed.
- Stir in raisins and candied orange peel and citron.
- Spoon filling into the partially baked pastry shell, spreading evenly with a rubber spatula.
6. Sprinkle slivered almonds or pine nuts on top, then weave or crisscross the pastry strips across the pie to create a lattice design.
7. Brush strips lightly with egg-white-water mixture.
8. Bake in the middle of the oven for 1 to 1 and 1/4 hours, or until the crust is golden and filling is firm.
9. Remove from oven and slide off the rim of the pan onto a wire rack. Serve on a serving plate.
Fresh fruits like white grapes serve as a tangy, sweet contrast to the richness of the pie. Crostata di ricotta is one of the oldest Roman dishes.
